items.title: 'Little disappointed in our batting unit': Miraz rues Tigers' ODI opener defeat against Zimbabwe
items.summary: Bangladesh captain Mehidy Hasan Miraz admitted he was "a little disappointed" with his side's batting after the Tigers slipped to a 25-run defeat in the first ODI against Zimbabwe in Harare on Monday, squandering a record-breaking bowling performance from Nahid Rana.

items.title: Batting woes waste Rana’s record-breaking performance
items.summary: A woeful batting display overshadowed a record-breaking bowling performance from Nahid Rana as Bangladesh suffered a 25-run defeat to Zimbabwe in the low-scoring first ODI of the three-match series at the Harare Sports Club on Monday.

items.title: Nyamhuri and Ngarava stun Bangladesh by defending 141
items.summary: Nahid Rana's 6 for 21, the best figures by a Bangladeshi in ODIs, went in vain

items.title: Pacers set up Zimbabwe's stunning defence of 141
items.summary: Nahid Rana's 6/21, the best ODI figures by a Bangladeshi bowler, vent in vain as Zimbabwe's pace quartet blew the visitors away for a famous win

items.title: Bangladesh bowl against Zimbabwe under slightly overcast skies
items.summary: Taskin Ahmed and Nahid Rana were back in Bangladesh's side while Raza returned to XI for Zimbabwe

items.title: Rana's five-for rocks Zimbabwe
items.summary: The Tigers enter the match looking to bounce back from their innings-and-85-run defeat in the one-off Test

items.title: Tigers bank on pacers after Test setback
items.summary: Bangladesh will head into the first of the three-match ODI series against Zimbabwe on Monday in Harare, hoping to put the disappointment of their innings-and-85-run defeat in the one-off Test behind them.

items.title: Bangladesh's big guns return for ODI battle against Zimbabwe
items.summary: Both teams will want to gain knowledge about their personnel in conditions where the 2027 World Cup will be played

items.title: Kaia, Burl, Marumani, Masakadza back in ODI side for Bangladesh series
items.summary: This will be Ngarava's first ODI series as captain, having recently led them to the one-off Test win against Bangladesh

items.title: Hridoy to lead Bangladesh in Zimbabwe T20Is; Saifuddin makes comeback
items.summary: Litton Das, Mehidy Hasan Miraz, Taskin Ahmed and Hasan Mahmud will be playing in the Lanka Premier League, which overlaps with the T20I series
